content: @supabase/supabase-js vs @supabase/ssr in Vite SPA: USE @supabase/supabase-js createClient() for a pure Vite SPA (no SSR). @supabase/ssr adds cookie coordination for SSR frameworks (Next.js/SvelteKit) — not needed in a Vite SPA. createBrowserClient from @supabase/ssr is essentially supabase-js with cookie support; for localStorage-only session management supabase-js is cleaner. Pattern: const supabase = createClient(VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY). Auth state: supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => {}). Get user: supabase.auth.getUser() (validates JWT; preferred over getSession which reads from storage without verification). Sign in: supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password }). On SIGNED_OUT or 401 from API: queryClient.clear() + navigate to /login. Current version: @supabase/supabase-js@2.108.2.
